rulings.Case Title: MARLA FISHER et al VS. IRMA ENCINAS et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 28, 2014, line 3. DEFENDANTs IRMA ENCINAS, ANDRES ALVARADO and SAUL ALVARADO's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Paragraphs 20, 28, 31, 26, and 57 are stricken with leave to amend to allege facts supporting malice, oppression or fraud. Paragraphs 9 and 10 are stricken with leave to amend to allege facts linking these allegations to Plaintiffs' causes of action. Motion is DENIED as to paragraph 69. Restitution (labeled as disgorgement in the complaint) is an available remedy per Korea Supply v. Lockhead (2003) 29 Cal.4th 1134. = (501/LKS)

rulings.Case Title: MARLA FISHER et al VS. IRMA ENCINAS et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 28, 2014, line 3. DEFENDANTs IRMA ENCINAS, ANDRES ALVARADO and SAUL ALVARADO's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with leave to amend: (1) fraud with particularity; and (2) intentional nuisance. Stoiber v. Honeychuck (1980) 101 Cal.App.3d 903. To the extent Plaintiff alleges negligent nuisance it is duplicative of the cause of action for negligence and demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Van Zyl v. Spielberg (1969) 2 Cal.App.3d 367; El Escorial Owner's Ass'n v. DLC Plastering (2007) 154 Cal.App.4th 1337. = (501/LKS)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID GREENE et al VS. PARK LANE ASSOCIATES LP, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 28, 2014, line 6. CROSS COMPLAINANT PARK LANE ASSOCIATES LP's DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO CROSS COMPLAINT SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend to allege facts in support of each affirmative defense. Opposing party cites no authority for the proposition that the sufficiency of allegation in the answer to cross-complaint may be evaluated based on supplementing it by allegations contained in the complaint. Demurrer is otherwise overruled, the fact that a party seeking declaratory relief is in pari delicto is a consideration which may be considered by the court as a sufficient reason for declining to grant declaratory relief. Moss v. Moss (1942) 20 Cal.2d 640. = (501/LKS)

rulings.Case Title: HOWARD L. WESTON VS. MIMI LAY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal The Record

rulings.Case Title: CARLOS MORA ET AL VS. CHRISTOPHER WILLITS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice, Motion, And Memorandum For Order Compelling Further Responses, Without Objections, To Plaintiff'S Form Interrogatories-- General To Christopher Willits- Set One; For Sanctions Of $2,160.00; And Attached Declaration Of Marc Branco
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 28, 2014, line 11. PLAINTIFFs CARLOS MORA and SANDRA MORA's Motion For Compelling Further Responses, Without Objections, To Plaintiff'S Form Interrogatories-- General To Christopher Willits- Set One; For Sanctions Of $2,160.00 DENIED. Form Interrogatories - General are not designed to be used as a part of unlimited number of form interrogatories in unlawful detainer cases. Even though defendant is not precluded from using such interrogatories in lieu of/or as a supplement to specially prepared interrogatories, there has been no declaration provided regarding the need for Special Interrogatories in excess of the statutory limit. No sanctions. = (501/LKS)
